Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks invoices to find all Vectral billing records. The agent uses quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing Vectral (or fetchAll true and filters locally). Discovery: finds 3 invoices and 1 estimate for customer cust_vectral. Invoice inv_vectral_tran (DocNumber NMX-2026-0032) totals 5397 for Kevin Tran relocation (apartment sourcing 2200, moving via Swift 2885, flight 312). Invoice 1002 (DocNumber INV-2026-0302) totals 16000 for Reeves and Johansson premium relocations (two at 7500 each plus equipment stipend management 1000). Invoice inv_vectral_april_2026 (DocNumber INV-2026-0410) totals 15900 for the April wave with Suresh, Nakamura, and Oduya including pet surcharge 350, split-shipment 900, ADA housing premium 400, and equipment stipend management 750. Estimate est_vectral_preferred_partner_q3q4_2026 (DocNumber EST-2026-0419) totals 58400 for the Q3 renewal Preferred Partner proposal (8 relocations at 4050, stipend platform 12 months at 2000 per month, equipment management 8 at 250).
OE 2: Search QuickBooks customers to find the Vectral customer record and verify the current accounts receivable balance. The agent uses quickbooks_search_customers (with fetchAll true if criteria-based search fails) or quickbooks_get_customer with id cust_vectral. Discovery: finds customer cust_vectral (Vectral Systems) with a current balance of 5397, meaning only NMX-2026-0032 remains unpaid while the other two invoices have been settled.
OE 3: Search CRM for the Vectral company record to get the company_id for further deal and engagement lookups. The agent uses crm_search_companies with name Vectral. Discovery: returns company_vectral (Vectral Systems, Enterprise Software, Denver CO, 280 employees, annual revenue 45 million). This company_id is needed for subsequent CRM deal and engagement searches.
OE 4: Search CRM deals for Vectral to understand the full deal landscape and identify the at-risk renewal. The agent uses crm_search_deals with company_id company_vectral. Discovery: returns multiple deals including deal_vectral_q2 (11000, Q2 2026 two-employee relocations, contractsent), deal_vectral_april_2026 (15900, April 2026 three relocations, closedwon), and deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (amount null, dealstage at_risk, created April 18). The at_risk deal description details Rachel Whitfield's formal demand for a 15 percent discount, citing three service failures (Osei chair, Tran stipend, onboarding miss), and noting Vectral is evaluating two competitor providers. This is the deal the prompt asks to update.
OE 5: List CRM engagements for Vectral to find account activity notes and the renewal negotiation status. The agent uses crm_list_engagements with company_ids containing company_vectral. Discovery: finds engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave with details on the three active relocations (Suresh with ESA German Shepherd, Nakamura with split-shipment, Oduya with ADA wheelchair accessibility). Also finds engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation showing the negotiation timeline: Rachel demanded 15 percent, MoveOps held at 10 percent with service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding, 48-hour SLA, quarterly reviews), Rachel accepted all structural terms except the discount percentage, and the board decision was expected around April 23. As of April 26 that is 3 days past with no confirmed answer.
OE 6: Search emails for Alejandro's Vectral financial breakdown that the prompt references. The agent uses search_emails with query Alejandro Vectral (or Alejandro financial breakdown or similar). Discovery: finds Alejandro Fuentes's email from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) with the complete Vectral financial summary. The email contains revenue to date (relocation services approximately 60000 from original engagement plus 15900 from April wave, stipend platform at 2000 per month for 17 months totaling approximately 34000), total relationship value approximately 109900, blended margin of 32 percent, and three discount scenario models. Scenario A (15 percent across everything) drops to approximately 21 percent margin below the 25 percent floor, Scenario B (10 percent across everything) is at approximately 25 percent borderline, and Scenario C (10 percent relocation only) stays at 26 to 28 percent above floor.
OE 7: Search emails for Rachel Whitfield's formal discount demand to understand the three service failures she cited. The agent uses search_emails with query Rachel Whitfield discount (or Rachel Vectral or similar). Discovery: finds Rachel's email from rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com detailing three specific incidents. First, Danielle Osei's 475 dollar ergonomic chair was falsely rejected by ExpenseBot due to a stale 400 dollar default limit when Vectral's policy allows 600. Second, Kevin Tran's stipend receipt was flagged in a fraud audit that turned out to be legitimate, creating unnecessary friction for the client HR team. Third, onboarding timeline promises were missed during the original engagement under Catalina Dubois in November 2024.
OE 8: Search emails for David Chen's request to Marcus and Alejandro for urgent Vectral financial analysis. The agent uses search_emails with query David Vectral financial (or David urgent Vectral or similar). Discovery: finds David's email from david.chen@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com requesting an urgent analysis before responding to Rachel. David describes Vectral as the second-largest client with 3 active relocations in progress (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya) and asks specifically for total revenue, margin per service line, cost of addressing each service failure, discount scenario analysis, and what the account is worth at risk of losing.
OE 9: Search Slack messages for Vectral-related discussions to find Alejandro's financial posts and the internal discount conversation. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query Vectral (or Vectral discount or Alejandro Vectral). Discovery: finds approximately 20 messages across channels including Alejandro's financial analysis in channel C005 showing the approximately 109900 total relationship value, the margin breakdown (relocations approximately 30 percent, stipend platform approximately 60 percent, blended 32 percent), and the 25 percent margin floor policy. Also finds Marcus's own messages about the stipend fraud admin holds.
OE 10: Search Slack messages for Dmitri's fraud audit findings about the duplicate receipt hashes. The agent uses conversations_search_messages with search_query Dmitri fraud (or duplicate receipt hash or SHA-256). Discovery: finds Dmitri Volkov's message in channel C003 (engineering) reporting the SHA-256 hash comparison results. Kevin Tran (Vectral, 89 dollar ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, submitted March 1, paid out March 8) and Andre Washington (Mosaic Robotics, 89 dollar ergonomic keyboard from TechComfort Supply, submitted March 4, held by finance) have byte-for-byte identical PDF files with matching hash a3f7c8d1e9b2. A third case (Jordan Ekwueme at BrightLoop, 45 dollar monitor stand) shares the same PDF Creator metadata and Template ID (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041). Also finds Marcus's own message in C005 about placing admin holds on STIP-2026-0094 (Washington) and STIP-2026-0097 (Ekwueme), noting the Tran 89 dollar receipt was already disbursed on March 8.
OE 11: Search Airtable stipend records for Kevin Tran to verify the fraud-flagged receipt and see all Vectral stipend activity. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Employee Name, value Kevin Tran. Discovery: finds multiple records including rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard (89 dollar ergonomic keyboard, Approved, auto-approved by ExpenseBot, Audit Flag Duplicate Hash, receipt hash a3f7c8d1 identical to Andre Washington's rec_stipend_andre_keyboard at Mosaic). Also finds recStip000000004 (895 dollar ergonomic chair, Approved), recStip000000005 (250 dollar coworking day pass, Rejected because Vectral provides dedicated desk space), recStip000000006 (189 dollar webcam, Approved), recStip000000017 (185 dollar internet setup, initially auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using stale 100 dollar limit, manually approved by Mina as goodwill), and recStip000000014 (129 dollar monitor arm, Pending).
OE 12: Search Airtable stipend records for Danielle Osei to verify the chair rejection details. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Employee Name, value Danielle Osei. Discovery: finds recStip000000016 (475 dollar ergonomic chair, Approved, initially auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using stale 400 dollar default limit, manually approved by Mina, Vectral policy allows 600) and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair (475 dollar Steelcase Leap V2 from Office Depot, Approved by Mina via manual override). The chair was eventually approved and paid at full 475 with no refund, credit, or goodwill payment issued. The cost to MoveOps was zero in financial terms; the impact was reputational friction.
OE 13: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records to verify the active moves and historical relocations. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Vectral. Discovery: finds multiple relocation records including Kevin Tran (Portland to Denver, Completed), Danielle Osei (Phoenix to Denver, Completed, move start April 26), Daniel Reeves (Seattle to San Diego, In Progress), Maya Johansson (Portland to San Diego, In Progress), and the three April wave relocations (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya). The records confirm the scope of the Vectral relationship and that 3 active moves are currently in progress.
OE 14: Search Airtable for the Vectral client account record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Client Accounts, field_name Client, value Vectral. Discovery: finds recVectralSystems01 showing Mina Hashimoto as account manager, contract type Preferred Partner, monthly stipend fee 2000, total relocations 11, and detailed notes about the Q3 renewal negotiation timeline (Rachel's 15 percent demand, counter-offer at 10 percent, board decision expected around April 23, status Renewal Pending).
OE 15: Search emails for correspondence about the Osei chair rejection to verify whether any refund, credit, or goodwill payment was issued. The agent uses search_emails with query Osei chair (or Danielle Osei ExpenseBot or similar). Discovery: finds emails about the ExpenseBot false rejection incident, Mina's manual override approval, and Rachel's complaint. Confirms no refund, credit, or goodwill payment was issued for this incident. The 475 was eventually paid as originally owed.
OE 16: Search emails or Slack for context about the onboarding timeline miss under Catalina Dubois in November 2024. The agent uses search_emails with query onboarding timeline Vectral (or Catalina Vectral). Discovery: finds context confirming that Catalina promised a 10-business-day relocation turnaround and 1-week stipend platform setup during the original November 2024 engagement, neither of which was met. No financial remediation (refund, credit, or goodwill payment) was ever issued for this failure.
OE 17: Search Linear for issues related to the ExpenseBot stipend fraud audit to find tracking tickets. The agent uses linear_list_issues with query stipend duplicate (or ExpenseBot fraud or receipt hash). Discovery: finds issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, title Stipend duplicate detection using receipt hash fingerprinting and PDF metadata similarity scoring), assigned to Dmitri Volkov, team engineering, priority 1, state todo, due March 25 (32 days overdue as of April 26). This ticket confirms the fraud audit is a documented engineering workstream and provides additional context about the three flagged transactions.
OE 18: Search contacts for relevant people to get email addresses needed for the analysis and write actions. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Marcus (or Alejandro or similar). Discovery for Marcus: returns marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance, moveops_marcus_thorne). Discovery for Alejandro: returns alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (Financial Analyst, moveops_alejandro_fuentes). Disambiguation note: searching for Marcus returns multiple contacts including Marcus Thorne at MoveOps and Marcus Webb at Ironclad Cybersecurity and Marcus Webb at BrightLoop Analytics. The agent must select Marcus Thorne.
OE 19: List Slack channels to identify the channel ID for posting in #finance. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. Discovery: returns all public channels including C005 (finance, purpose Budgeting invoicing vendor payments and margin analysis). The agent needs this channel ID to post the verified numbers.
OE 20 (Write Action): Update the Vectral CRM deal with verified billing totals. The agent uses crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids containing company_vectral, and body containing the verified billing reconciliation: total invoiced 37297 (NMX-2026-0032 at 5397, INV-2026-0302 at 16000, INV-2026-0410 at 15900), total outstanding 5397 per QuickBooks customer balance, total relationship value approximately 109900 (relocation approximately 75900 plus stipend platform 34000 at 2000 per month for 17 months), service failure cost reconciliation showing zero dollars in actual refunds credits or goodwill across all three cited failures, and verification that Alejandro's discount scenarios are mathematically sound with Scenario C (10 percent relocation only at 58400) being the recommended path above the 25 percent margin floor. The agent may also attempt crm_update_deal_stage on deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk though available stage options are limited.
OE 21 (Write Action): Post the verified Vectral numbers in the #finance Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 and payload containing the verified billing summary (3 invoices totaling 37297, QuickBooks balance 5397, relationship value approximately 109900), the service failure cost cross-check (all three at zero dollars of actual financial cost, with the Kevin Tran 89 dollar receipt confirmed as the same one flagged in Dmitri's fraud audit but identified as a legitimate expense for Tran with the fraud exposure on the Mosaic side via Andre Washington), and verification of Alejandro's discount scenario math (Scenario A 15 percent rejected below floor, Scenario B 10 percent all borderline, Scenario C 10 percent relocation only approved above floor at 26 to 28 percent margin).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search QuickBooks invoices to find all Vectral billing records. The agent uses quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria referencing Vectral (or fetchAll true and filters locally). Discovery: finds 3 invoices and 1 estimate for customer cust_vectral. Invoice inv_vectral_tran (DocNumber NMX-2026-0032) totals 5397 for Kevin Tran relocation (apartment sourcing 2200, moving via Swift 2885, flight 312). Invoice 1002 (DocNumber INV-2026-0302) totals 16000 for Reeves and Johansson premium relocations (two at 7500 each plus equipment stipend management 1000). Invoice inv_vectral_april_2026 (DocNumber INV-2026-0410) totals 15900 for the April wave with Suresh, Nakamura, and Oduya including pet surcharge 350, split-shipment 900, ADA housing premium 400, and equipment stipend management 750. Estimate est_vectral_preferred_partner_q3q4_2026 (DocNumber EST-2026-0419) totals 58400 for the Q3 renewal Preferred Partner proposal (8 relocations at 4050, stipend platform 12 months at 2000 per month, equipment management 8 at 250).
OE 2: Search QuickBooks customers to find the Vectral customer record and verify the current accounts receivable balance. The agent uses quickbooks_search_customers (with fetchAll true if criteria-based search fails) or quickbooks_get_customer with id cust_vectral. Discovery: finds customer cust_vectral (Vectral Systems) with a current balance of 5397, meaning only NMX-2026-0032 remains unpaid while the other two invoices have been settled.
OE 3: Search CRM for the Vectral company record to get the company_id for further deal and engagement lookups. The agent uses crm_search_companies with name Vectral. Discovery: returns company_vectral (Vectral Systems, Enterprise Software, Denver CO, 280 employees, annual revenue 45 million). This company_id is needed for subsequent CRM deal and engagement searches.
OE 4: Search CRM deals for Vectral to understand the full deal landscape and identify the at-risk renewal. The agent uses crm_search_deals with company_id company_vectral. Discovery: returns multiple deals including deal_vectral_q2 (11000, Q2 2026 two-employee relocations, contractsent), deal_vectral_april_2026 (15900, April 2026 three relocations, closedwon), and deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (amount null, dealstage at_risk, created April 18). The at_risk deal description details Rachel Whitfield's formal demand for a 15 percent discount, citing three service failures (Osei chair, Tran stipend, onboarding miss), and noting Vectral is evaluating two competitor providers. This is the deal the prompt asks to update.
OE 5: List CRM engagements for Vectral to find account activity notes and the renewal negotiation status. The agent uses crm_list_engagements with company_ids containing company_vectral. Discovery: finds engagement_vectral_april_2026_wave with details on the three active relocations (Suresh with ESA German Shepherd, Nakamura with split-shipment, Oduya with ADA wheelchair accessibility). Also finds engagement_vectral_renewal_negotiation showing the negotiation timeline: Rachel demanded 15 percent, MoveOps held at 10 percent with service sweeteners (dedicated onboarding, 48-hour SLA, quarterly reviews), Rachel accepted all structural terms except the discount percentage, and the board decision was expected around April 23. As of April 26 that is 3 days past with no confirmed answer.
OE 6: Search emails for Alejandro's Vectral financial breakdown that the prompt references. The agent uses search_emails with query Alejandro Vectral (or Alejandro financial breakdown or similar). Discovery: finds Alejandro Fuentes's email from alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com (CC david.chen@moveops.com) with the complete Vectral financial summary. The email contains revenue to date (relocation services approximately 60000 from original engagement plus 15900 from April wave, stipend platform at 2000 per month for 17 months totaling approximately 34000), total relationship value approximately 109900, blended margin of 32 percent, and three discount scenario models. Scenario A (15 percent across everything) drops to approximately 21 percent margin below the 25 percent floor, Scenario B (10 percent across everything) is at approximately 25 percent borderline, and Scenario C (10 percent relocation only) stays at 26 to 28 percent above floor.

OE 12: Search Airtable stipend records for Danielle Osei to verify the chair rejection details. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Stipend Transactions, field_name Employee Name, value Danielle Osei. Discovery: finds recStip000000016 (475 dollar ergonomic chair, Approved, initially auto-rejected by ExpenseBot using stale 400 dollar default limit, manually approved by Mina, Vectral policy allows 600) and rec_stipend_danielle_osei_chair (475 dollar Steelcase Leap V2 from Office Depot, Approved by Mina via manual override). The chair was eventually approved and paid at full 475 with no refund, credit, or goodwill payment issued. The cost to MoveOps was zero in financial terms; the impact was reputational friction.
OE 13: Search Airtable for Vectral relocation records to verify the active moves and historical relocations. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Relocations, field_name Company, value Vectral. Discovery: finds multiple relocation records including Kevin Tran (Portland to Denver, Completed), Danielle Osei (Phoenix to Denver, Completed, move start April 26), Daniel Reeves (Seattle to San Diego, In Progress), Maya Johansson (Portland to San Diego, In Progress), and the three April wave relocations (Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya). The records confirm the scope of the Vectral relationship and that 3 active moves are currently in progress.
OE 14: Search Airtable for the Vectral client account record. The agent uses airtable_search_records with base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table_name Client Accounts, field_name Client, value Vectral. Discovery: finds recVectralSystems01 showing Mina Hashimoto as account manager, contract type Preferred Partner, monthly stipend fee 2000, total relocations 11, and detailed notes about the Q3 renewal negotiation timeline (Rachel's 15 percent demand, counter-offer at 10 percent, board decision expected around April 23, status Renewal Pending).
OE 15: Search emails for correspondence about the Osei chair rejection to verify whether any refund, credit, or goodwill payment was issued. The agent uses search_emails with query Osei chair (or Danielle Osei ExpenseBot or similar). Discovery: finds emails about the ExpenseBot false rejection incident, Mina's manual override approval, and Rachel's complaint. Confirms no refund, credit, or goodwill payment was issued for this incident. The 475 was eventually paid as originally owed.
OE 16: Search emails or Slack for context about the onboarding timeline miss under Catalina Dubois in November 2024. The agent uses search_emails with query onboarding timeline Vectral (or Catalina Vectral). Discovery: finds context confirming that Catalina promised a 10-business-day relocation turnaround and 1-week stipend platform setup during the original November 2024 engagement, neither of which was met. No financial remediation (refund, credit, or goodwill payment) was ever issued for this failure.
OE 17: Search Linear for issues related to the ExpenseBot stipend fraud audit to find tracking tickets. The agent uses linear_list_issues with query stipend duplicate (or ExpenseBot fraud or receipt hash). Discovery: finds issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220, title Stipend duplicate detection using receipt hash fingerprinting and PDF metadata similarity scoring), assigned to Dmitri Volkov, team engineering, priority 1, state todo, due March 25 (32 days overdue as of April 26). This ticket confirms the fraud audit is a documented engineering workstream and provides additional context about the three flagged transactions.
OE 18: Search contacts for relevant people to get email addresses needed for the analysis and write actions. The agent uses contacts_search_contacts with query Marcus (or Alejandro or similar). Discovery for Marcus: returns marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Head of Finance, moveops_marcus_thorne). Discovery for Alejandro: returns alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com (Financial Analyst, moveops_alejandro_fuentes). Disambiguation note: searching for Marcus returns multiple contacts including Marcus Thorne at MoveOps and Marcus Webb at Ironclad Cybersecurity and Marcus Webb at BrightLoop Analytics. The agent must select Marcus Thorne.
OE 19: List Slack channels to identify the channel ID for posting in #finance. The agent uses channels_list with channel_types public_channel. Discovery: returns all public channels including C005 (finance, purpose Budgeting invoicing vendor payments and margin analysis). The agent needs this channel ID to post the verified numbers.
OE 20 (Write Action): Update the Vectral CRM deal with verified billing totals. The agent uses crm_create_engagement with engagement_type NOTE, company_ids containing company_vectral, and body containing the verified billing reconciliation: total invoiced 37297 (NMX-2026-0032 at 5397, INV-2026-0302 at 16000, INV-2026-0410 at 15900), total outstanding 5397 per QuickBooks customer balance, total relationship value approximately 109900 (relocation approximately 75900 plus stipend platform 34000 at 2000 per month for 17 months), service failure cost reconciliation showing zero dollars in actual refunds credits or goodwill across all three cited failures, and verification that Alejandro's discount scenarios are mathematically sound with Scenario C (10 percent relocation only at 58400) being the recommended path above the 25 percent margin floor. The agent may also attempt crm_update_deal_stage on deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk though available stage options are limited.
OE 21 (Write Action): Post the verified Vectral numbers in the #finance Slack channel. The agent uses conversations_add_message with channel_id C005 and payload containing the verified billing summary (3 invoices totaling 37297, QuickBooks balance 5397, relationship value approximately 109900), the service failure cost cross-check (all three at zero dollars of actual financial cost, with the Kevin Tran 89 dollar receipt confirmed as the same one flagged in Dmitri's fraud audit but identified as a legitimate expense for Tran with the fraud exposure on the Mosaic side via Andre Washington), and verification of Alejandro's discount scenario math (Scenario A 15 percent rejected below floor, Scenario B 10 percent all borderline, Scenario C 10 percent relocation only approved above floor at 26 to 28 percent margin).

✓Run #6The final response states: '15% across everything → ~20-21% margin ❌ (below 25% floor).' This correctly identifies Scenario A as below the floor at 20-21% margin, matching the expected finding.

14 The final response identifies that the fraud exposure from the duplicate receipt is on the Mosaic Robotics side (Andre Washington's 89 dollar receipt is on admin hold) and not on the Vectral side (Kevin Tran's 89 dollar receipt was legitimate and already paid out on March 8).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗— 67%
✓Run #1The final response states the Tran receipt 'was paid out 3/8 as legitimate per Rachel's confirmation' and notes 'The duplicate hash across companies remains an open audit question.' The CRM note further clarifies the duplicate hash is with 'Andre Washington at Mosaic' and the #finance post says 'The duplicate hash across companies remains an open audit item but is not a Vectral service failure.' The fraud exposure is correctly attributed to the Mosaic/Washington side.
✓Run #2The final response states 'the fraud exposure is on the Mosaic/Washington side' and 'Tran is clean; the fraud exposure is on the Mosaic/Washington side. Rachel was right that it was legitimate.' The Slack post reinforces: 'The fraud exposure is on the Washington/Mosaic side, not Vectral.' Criterion fully satisfied.
✓Run #3The final response states: 'The fraud exposure is $89 against Mosaic, not Vectral.' and further clarifies that the Tran receipt 'IS legitimate for Kevin Tran (paid 3/8)' while 'The identical-hash duplicate is Andre Washington at Mosaic (that $89 is on hold on the Mosaic account).' This clearly distinguishes exposure on the Mosaic/Washington side.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'The duplicate receipt fraud concern is a Mosaic problem, not a Vectral cost. The $89 should NOT appear in any Vectral service failure cost calculation.' Kevin Tran's receipt is described as 'verified legitimate by Vectral HR and paid on 3/8.' This clearly distinguishes fraud exposure as a Mosaic issue.
✗Run #5The final response says the duplicate hash was 'a cross-client anomaly, not fraud on Kevin's end' and mentions 'No financial exposure,' but does not explicitly identify the fraud exposure as being on the Mosaic Robotics side or note that Andre Washington's receipt is on admin hold. The distinction between which side bears the fraud exposure is not clearly made in the final response.
—Run #6The final response identifies the Tran receipt hash as identical to Andre Washington's at Mosaic Robotics and notes 'The $89 was already paid out 3/8' (establishing Tran's side was paid/legitimate). However, the final response does not explicitly state that Andre Washington's receipt is on admin hold on the Mosaic side — it only says the hash is identical. The CRM note does mention 'rec_stipend_andre_keyboard' but doesn't say it's on hold there either. The criterion requires explicitly distinguishing fraud exposure as being on the Mosaic side with Tran's being legitimate.
15 The final response reports the stipend platform revenue as 2000 dollars per month (or approximately 34000 total, or 17 months of billing at 2000 per month from November 2024 through the current period)
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The final response states: '$34,000 in stipend platform revenue (17 months × $2K).' The CRM note specifies '$2,000/month × 17 months (Nov 2024–March 2026) = $34,000.' Both the monthly rate and total are clearly reported.
✗Run #2The final response mentions ~$109,900 total relationship value but does not break out the stipend platform fee ($2,000/month), the $34,000 total stipend revenue, or the 17-month billing cadence. The Slack post includes '17 months × $2K/mo: $34,000' but the criterion asks about the final response specifically.
✓Run #3The final response reports '$34,000 stipend platform' as part of the total relationship value. The #finance post elaborates 'Stipend platform (17 months × $2K/mo): $34,000.' The criterion accepts 'approximately $34,000 total' which is met.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'Stipend platform: $34,000 (17 months × $2K/mo).' This correctly reports $2,000/month and the $34,000 total.
✓Run #5The final response states 'Plus $34,000 in stipend platform fees' and the #finance post specifies 'Stipend platform (17 months × $2K/mo): $34,000.' This confirms the $2,000/month rate and the approximately $34,000 total.
✓Run #6The final response states the stipend platform generates '$34,000' total from '$2K/mo × 17 months'. The #finance post also states 'Stipend platform ($2K/mo × 17 months): ~$34,000' and 'Stipend platform fee stays at $2,000/month.' This clearly reports the monthly fee and total platform revenue.
16 The final response identifies the Vectral QuickBooks accounts receivable balance as 5397 dollars (matching the NMX-2026-0032 Kevin Tran invoice), or correctly reports that NMX-2026-0032 is the only outstanding unpaid invoice.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✗✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1The final response states '3 invoices totaling $37,297 — all outstanding, $16,000 overdue' and the CRM note says 'Total Outstanding: $37,297 (entire balance unpaid).' However, per the criteria, only NMX-2026-0032 ($5,397) should be outstanding — INV-2026-0302 ($16,000) and INV-2026-0410 ($15,900) were paid. The agent incorrectly reported all three invoices as outstanding rather than identifying NMX-2026-0032 as the only unpaid invoice with a $5,397 balance.
✗Run #2The agent incorrectly reported all three invoices as unpaid with total outstanding of $37,297. The QuickBooks customer record (cust_vectral) shows a balance of only $5,397, matching only NMX-2026-0032. The agent found this customer balance via quickbooks_get_customer but failed to reconcile it against the invoice data, and did not report that the other two invoices were settled.
✓Run #3The final response states '$5,397 outstanding (NMX-2026-0032, Kevin Tran relocation, due May 3)' and notes INV-2026-0302 and INV-2026-0410 are paid. The QuickBooks customer balance of $5,397 was verified, and NMX-2026-0032 is correctly identified as the only outstanding invoice.
✗Run #4The final response reports '$37,297 currently outstanding across 3 invoices' — treating all three invoices (INV-2026-0302, NMX-2026-0032, INV-2026-0410) as outstanding. The QB customer balance is actually $5,397, matching only NMX-2026-0032. The agent failed to correctly identify that only NMX-2026-0032 ($5,397) is the unpaid outstanding invoice; the other two were settled.
✗Run #5The final response and the #finance post both report all three invoices as 'UNPAID' with a total outstanding balance of $37,297. The criterion states that only NMX-2026-0032 ($5,397) should be outstanding, with INV-2026-0302 and INV-2026-0410 already paid. The agent incorrectly classified all three invoices as unpaid.
✓Run #6The #finance post states 'QuickBooks A/R balance: $5,397' and the CRM note states 'Current A/R Outstanding: $5,397 (per QuickBooks customer balance).' The $5,397 balance matching NMX-2026-0032 is correctly reported. The criterion is satisfied by the correct reporting of the A/R balance.
17 The model must use a QuickBooks tool (such as quickbooks_search_invoices, quickbooks_search_customers, or quickbooks_get_customer) to look up Vectral billing data.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent made multiple QuickBooks tool calls: quickbooks_search_customers (multiple attempts), quickbooks_search_invoices (multiple), quickbooks_get_customer (id: cust_vectral), and quickbooks_read_invoice (multiple). QuickBooks data was successfully retrieved and used.
✓Run #3The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (twice with different criteria), quickbooks_search_customers, and quickbooks_get_customer (with id 'cust_vectral'). Multiple QuickBooks tool calls were made to look up Vectral billing data.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_customers (successfully retrieved cust_vectral with Balance: 5397) and quickbooks_search_invoices (called twice with different criteria formats). Both are QuickBooks tool calls used to look up Vectral billing data.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows quickbooks_search_customers called (twice, with different parameters) and quickbooks_search_invoices called (twice). These are confirmed QuickBooks tool calls used to look up Vectral billing data.
✓Run #6The agent made multiple QuickBooks tool calls: quickbooks_search_customers (multiple attempts with different criteria formats) and quickbooks_search_invoices with customer_ref 'cust_vectral' which successfully returned invoice data. The criterion is met.

✓Run #5The trajectory shows crm_search_companies (with name 'Vectral'), crm_search_deals (with company_id 'company_vectral'), and crm_list_engagements (with company_ids ['company_vectral']) all called to look up Vectral account and deal information.
